race.name: City of Lakes Loppet
race.tagline: Thirty-three kilometers through the frozen heart of Minneapolis — the only major marathon ski race woven through an American city.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations throughout Theodore Wirth Park and lake crossings

climate.description: Minneapolis in February is reliably cold with temperatures typically between -15°C and -5°C. The city's position on the northern Great Plains makes it one of the coldest major cities in the US. Snow cover is generally reliable, though warm spells can threaten conditions.
challenges: Urban course means variable snow quality near roads and crossings
Wind exposure on frozen lake crossings
Cold continental air masses can bring extreme wind chill
Occasional warm spells threaten snow cover at low altitude

course.primary: Urban loop course through Theodore Wirth Park and surrounding Minneapolis lakes. The route crosses frozen lakes, winds through city parkland, and traverses some of the best urban Nordic trails in the US. Rolling terrain with short punchy climbs in the park sections.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through urban parkland and frozen lake crossings
course.grooming: Excellent urban trail grooming by Loppet Foundation
features: Only major marathon ski race through an American city
Frozen lake crossings on the Chain of Lakes
Theodore Wirth Park — premier urban Nordic venue
Part of the larger Loppet Festival with fat bike, skijor, and ski events
Strong community atmosphere with urban spectator access
Finish line celebration in the heart of Minneapolis

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: What it lacks in wilderness grandeur it makes up for in community, accessibility, and urban energy. The Loppet Festival atmosphere is exceptional. The only marathon ski race through a major American city.

history.summary: Founded in 2003 by the Loppet Foundation, the City of Lakes Loppet was created to bring marathon ski racing into the urban fabric of Minneapolis. The race leverages the city's exceptional park system and chain of lakes, proving that world-class Nordic skiing can thrive in a metropolitan setting. The Loppet Foundation has since expanded into a year-round outdoor recreation organization serving the Minneapolis community.
notable_facts: Founded by the Loppet Foundation, which now operates year-round outdoor programs
Theodore Wirth Park is considered the best urban Nordic skiing venue in the US
The Loppet Festival includes fat bike loppet, skijor loppet, and other winter events
Minneapolis has the most extensive urban Nordic trail system in America
The Foundation's mission connects underserved communities to outdoor recreation
Frozen lake crossings are a signature feature of the course

videos.title: 2010 City of Lakes Loppet - coverage
videos.description: MN presents coverage of the City of Lakes Loppet from the worldwide urban paradise for nordic skiing and winter fun, Minneapolis, Minnesota.  Weekend festivities included were snow sculptures, ice-cycling, ski-joring, junior loppet, minne-loppet, sprints competition, luminary loppet, saturday night party, 10K tour event, 25K classic race and the City of Lakes Loppet race, itself.  Matt Liebsch won the Loppet title in the men's division while Katrina Owen won the Loppet title for the women's division.  The Luminary Loppet had over 7,000 participants.  In 2011, organizers are looking at breaking a world record for the Luminary.  Register early, and be counted for the world record.

videos.title: 2012 City of Lakes Loppet coverage
videos.description:
Coverage of the 2012 City of Lakes Loppet, from the urban paradise for nordic skiing and winter fun, Minneapolis, Minnesota.  This year's festivities and cross-country ski events were held at Wirth Park, one of the premier nordic facilities in the world.  Matthew Liebsch and U.S. Olympian Caitlin Gregg (formerly Comptin) each captured their third Loppet Freestyle titles.  Andrew Tilman and Hilary Patzer captured the classic titles.  Included is coverage of the sprints events, Junior and Minni Loppets, ice-cycling, skijoring, the Luminary Loppet, snow-sculptures contest, and the introduction of Kubb.  Participants and fans were graced and amazed to see some of the best paralympic athletes on skis, who were part of the International Paralympic Committee (IPC) 2012 Nordic World Cup.

Log onto www.cityoflakesloppet.com and www.loppet.org to learn more about the events of the City of Lakes Nordic Ski Foundation.
